title: Tender Smoked Venezuelan Peanuts Grill
description: A winey, comforting Venezuelan dish featuring peanuts, with a nice cheese pull, perfect for a macrobiotic-friendly meal on a robata grill.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat (400°F).
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, apple cider vinegar, honey, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, smoked paprika, black pepper, and salt.
Add the peanuts to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the marinade.
Thread the marinated peanuts onto skewers, leaving a small space between each peanut.
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter on a piece of aluminum foil and place it on the grill.
Place a few skewers of peanuts onto the foiled butter and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until slightly charred.
Meanwhile, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add the gruyere cheese and stir until melted and smooth.
Remove the peanuts from the grill and brush with the melted cheese mixture.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
Serve hot with additional cheese sauce for dipping, if desired.
